SSP SLAM — the shipped recipe, liveoctave polar lattice · range gating · rotation = lattice permutation

–fps
–match ms
–est err m
–odom err m
–est rmse m
–closures
96D (complex)
0keyframe
kf 0 / 0
Lattice writes this frame — rings × angles disc, outer = fine λ. Range gating blanks the fine (outer) rings for far samples.
Correlation surface — Re<M, shift(d)·Sθ> over the coarse translation window at the winning θ (hover: θ candidates via exact lattice permutation). White ring = picked peak, cross = motion-prior guess.
Decoded map — Re<M, φ(x)> heatmap, a few rows per frame (Intel: the sliding local-map bundle, window follows the robot; sandbox: the full room). Blue = surface evidence, red = negative sidelobes. Watch phantom walls appear with the occlusion filter off, and fine detail blur with gating off.

Drive (sandbox)

1.1 m/s
Arrow keys steer (switches to manual). The estimator only sees the lidar — constant-velocity guess + correlation search, as in ssp_slam.py.

Replay (Intel log)

The full bounded-memory stack (ssp_bounded.py, shipped config): rigid anchor-frame segment vectors + d/dθ derivatives (query rotation = exact permutation + first-order correction), recency-windowed frontend (12 anchors / 8 m), pass-segregated loop constraints (gap 300 kf, radius 5 m), session-relative soft coherence response (target 0.55), drift-scaled innovation gate (χ²>9), Cauchy IRLS + leave-one-out pruning, damped Gauss-Newton with analytic SE(2) Jacobian capped at ~30 evaluations — the early stop IS the regularizer (RESULTS.md, shipped block). Relaxations are amortized across frames ("relaxing graph…").

Range gating

β 3.0
A sample at range r writes ring λ only if λ ≥ β·r·Δθ. This is a rule for world-frame map sums (R1 law) — live in the sandbox map writes. On the Intel tab the shipped segment folds are deliberately UNGATED (rigid anchor-frame segments have no cross-pass aliasing; gating the fold costs 9.2→14 cm, R2), so there the toggle drives the ray coloring + write-disc visualization of what a tiered world-frame map would receive.

Scale ladder

Finding 7: φ is the additive-resonance ratio (φ²=φ+1) — ring-magnitude difference-beats (|wi|−|wj|=|wm|) put sidelobes in the radial correlation kernel. The hazard is environment-coupled: flat walls (sandbox) expose the beats along wall normals — watch drift jump — while on cluttered Intel geometry the extra scale density can even win (measured on this subset: octaves 2.0 m ATE vs φ 0.14 m with 2.3× the closures); both halves are in finding 7. Ladder = 0.25·φk, same [0.25, 2] band, 5 rings. Octaves sit on the safe r≈1.4–2.8 plateau. Switching resets the map.

Occlusion filter

Finding 8: kills phantom bridges across depth discontinuities (red segments). Off: the bridges are encoded — phantom walls grow in the decoded map. (The naive |Δr|>2|Δp| can never fire — reverse triangle inequality.)

Sensor noise (sandbox)

σ 2.0 cm
drop 2%
Shipped sim: σ=2 cm, 2% dropout. Finding 5: λmin ~ noise scale — crank σ past the 0.25 m ring's usefulness and fine registration degrades.

Lattice angles

Sandbox only — the Intel pipeline runs the shipped 4×60 lattice (D=240) throughout. Finding 6: angle density is environment-coupled — long flat walls only excite frequencies near their normals; 24 (7.5° spacing) is the knee in rotated worlds. Switching resets the sandbox map.
What am I looking at?
  • The map is a handful of complex vectors (D=96 features by default), not an occupancy grid. Each lidar sample point p contributes a phasor bundle φ(p)=exp(i W p); a map vector is the weighted sum of many of them. W is a deterministic polar lattice: wavelength rings (0.25/0.5/1/2 m octaves) × 24 equally spaced angles — the winning recipe of the encoder sweep in RESULTS.md (0.9 cm mean error at 1/21 the dimensions of random Fourier features).
  • Translation is phase multiplication: φ(p+d) = eiWd·φ(p). Scan-to-map registration is the correlation Re<M, eiWdSθ> on a coarse-to-fine grid of (θ, d) around a motion-prior guess — the small heatmap is that surface, live, with parabolic sub-cell refinement.
  • Rotation is an exact permutation of the lattice's angle index (conjugate on the 180° sector wrap), so coarse θ candidates cost two encodes total instead of one per angle — the trick shipped as the "perm matcher" in ssp_bounded.py.
  • Intel replay: the classic Intel Research Lab log (13,631 SICK scans, 180 beams / 180°) keyframed at 0.10 m / 5°, run through the full bounded-memory SLAM stack (ssp_bounded.py): each keyframe folds immediately into a rigid anchor-frame segment vector + d/dθ derivative vector (5-kf segments; query-time anchor rotation = nearest-3° lattice permutation + first-order derivative correction — HY4 matched-band semantics, D=240); the frontend matches recent segments only; old passes act through gated loop constraints; an anchor pose graph (sequential edges 0.03 m/0.3°, 0.10/1.5 on odometry-fallback spans) is relaxed by damped Gauss-Newton with an analytic SE(2) Jacobian, Cauchy IRLS and leave-one-out pruning, capped at ~30 evaluations per solve — the truncation is the audited load-bearing regularizer. Gray = raw odometry dead reckoning, yellow = graph-corrected SSP estimate, green = RBPF-corrected reference, magenta = accepted closures. The "ate rmse" tile is Horn-aligned against the reference, matching the Python eval (align_se2); instantaneous "est err"/"odom err" are start-frame anchored.
  • Range gating: at range r the beam spacing Δθ under-samples wavelengths finer than ≈r·Δθ, so far samples only write the coarse rings (λ ≥ βrΔθ) of the map. The disc panel shows each frame's write pattern — fine (outer) rings blank out for far geometry. Matching stays ungated (R2: gating the matched band costs accuracy).
  • Occlusion filter: consecutive-beam hits are bridged into wall segments unless the range jump exceeds 2× the tangential gap — near-radial "bridges" (red) are phantom walls across depth edges, not surfaces.
  • Decode panel: a map vector is decoded by correlating M with φ(x) on a grid — the map "emerging" is literally interference of the stored phasors.
Controls — Intel tab: Space play/pause · scrub bar seeks (backward seek replays from the start) · speed = keyframes per rendered frame. Sandbox tab: ↑↓ speed, ←→ steer (switches to manual) · A autopilot · Space pause · R reset map. Append ?test=1 to run the built-in self-tests (shift = phase multiply, rotation = lattice permutation, occlusion identity, range gating, end-to-end matcher, SE(2) composition, pose-graph solver, segment fold + derivative correction, embedded-data integrity). Faithful JS port of ssp_slam.py (encoder/matcher) + ssp_slam_loop.py (ring-major lattice, rot_permute) + ssp_bounded.py/ssp_bounded_carmen.py (segments, coherence response, innovation gate, IRLS/LOO backend, shipped config); findings cited from RESULTS.md. Data: data/intel.log (Radish / StachnissLab, D. Haehnel), embedded by demo/export_data.py.
